# Principles from Van Gogh's Life

**Principle 1: Art Is a Calling. Van Gogh could not stop painting. It was his reason for being.**

**Principle 2: Suffering Is Part of Creation. The novel shows the cost of artistic genius.**

**Principle 3: Support Matters. Theo's belief kept Vincent going.**

**Case: The Rejection. The art world rejected van Gogh. He kept painting.**

**Case: The Poverty. Vincent was poor his whole life. He painted anyway.**

**Case: The Recognition. After his death, van Gogh became one of the most famous artists.**

**Case: The Call to Art. Vincent wrote: I want to touch people with my art. I want them to say: he feels deeply, he feels tenderly.**
**Case: The Failure. Vincent failed at everything except art. He was fired from his job, rejected by the church, abandoned by lovers.**
**Case: The Obsession. Vincent painted continuously. He completed 900 paintings in ten years. Almost one every four days.**
**Case: The Faith. Vincent's religious faith transformed into artistic faith. Painting became his religion.**
**Case: The Letters. Over 600 letters between Vincent and Theo survive. They are among the greatest correspondence in history.**
**Case: The Despair. Vincent wrote: I risk my life for my work. My sanity has been half destroyed.**
